platforms.name: Charles Schwab
strengths: No account minimums
thinkorswim platform integrated (post-TD Ameritrade merger)
24/7 world-class customer service
Wide range of investment options including fractional shares
weaknesses: Platform interface can be overwhelming for beginners
Options fees remain higher than modern zero-fee competitors
platforms.targetInvestor: Investors seeking comprehensive research tools, top-tier platforms, and long-term investing support

platforms.name: Fidelity Investments
strengths: Outstanding research capabilities
Uninvested cash automatically sweeps to high-yield core position
Fractional shares available for stocks and ETFs
Zero expense ratio mutual funds available
weaknesses: Active futures traders may prefer other platforms
Mobile UI can feel slightly dated compared to fintech startups
platforms.targetInvestor: Buy-and-hold investors focused on retirement, compound interest, and wealth building

platforms.name: E*TRADE by Morgan Stanley
strengths: Power E*TRADE platform is elite for derivatives
Strong options trading tools with volume discounts ($0.50/contract)
Backed by Morgan Stanley institutional research
Competitive margin rates
weaknesses: Some advanced screening tools require higher account balances
platforms.targetInvestor: Active traders and options enthusiasts seeking professional-grade tools

platforms.name: Robinhood
strengths: Extremely simple, gamified interface
No options contract fees
1-3% matching on IRA retirement contributions (with Gold subscription)
24/5 trading capabilities and crypto access
weaknesses: Limited advanced charting and research tools
Customer service relies heavily on in-app chat
Payment for Order Flow (PFOF) model raises execution quality concerns for large orders
platforms.targetInvestor: First-time investors and cost-conscious options traders wanting a simple, mobile-first experience

platforms.name: Webull
strengths: Advanced charting natively built into mobile and desktop
Zero commission on options trading
Robust pre-market and after-hours trading hours
Paper trading available for strategy testing
weaknesses: Steep learning curve for absolute beginners
Customer support is less robust than legacy brokers
platforms.targetInvestor: Technical traders seeking advanced charting and zero-fee options without the legacy institutional overhead

platforms.name: Interactive Brokers
strengths: Lowest margin rates in the industry
Access to global markets across dozens of countries
Advanced algorithmic order types
No inactivity fees
weaknesses: TWS Desktop platform has an incredibly steep learning curve
Pricing tiers (Lite vs. Pro) can be confusing to navigate
Overkill for simple buy-and-hold index investors
platforms.targetInvestor: Sophisticated, high-net-worth traders needing international market access, low margin rates, and algorithmic execution
